title: psephellus ciscaucasicus
intro: Psephellus ciscaucasicus, also known as Caucasian scabious or Caucasian psathyrella, is a plant species in the daisy family native to the Caucasus region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Psephellus ciscaucasicus is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal dosage.
